notes: Pinned OCI SDK contract: CreateDelegationControl, GetDelegationControl, ListDelegationControls, ChangeDelegationControlCompartment, UpdateDelegationControl, DeleteDelegationControl, and GetWorkRequest are present in v65.110.0. Create and update return a DelegationControl body plus opc-work-request-id; delete returns only request and work-request headers. ListDelegationControls filters compartmentId, lifecycleState, displayName, resourceType, and resourceId and returns Items []DelegationControlSummary.

Pinned provider coverage is partial. The onboarding audit confirmed the published resource type oci_delegate_access_control_delegation_control and an accessible singular DelegationControl datasource, but it did not confirm a separate plural datasource page or waiter implementation at the pinned provider boundary. The listLookup datasource and collection metadata therefore stay conservative OCI-style placeholders until provider-source confirmation is available, and the mutability overlay uses a checked-in docs fixture because the live Terraform Registry resource page is JavaScript-only.

Repo-authored runtime contract stays narrower than the imported SDK surface: only displayName, description, numApprovalsRequired, delegationSubscriptionIds, isAutoApproveDuringMaintenance, resourceIds, preApprovedServiceProviderActionNames, notificationTopicId, notificationMessageFormat, freeformTags, and definedTags reconcile in place. ChangeDelegationControlCompartment remains out of scope, while compartmentId, resourceType, vaultId, and vaultKeyId stay replacement-only drift.

Repo-authored bind-versus-create stays explicit: when no OCI identifier is tracked, the runtime only attempts reuse with exact compartmentId, displayName, resourceType, and a reusable resourceId filter, then rereads candidates through GetDelegationControl and reuses only a unique candidate whose full resourceIds set and create-only vault inputs still match.

Delegate Access Control work requests expose CREATE_DELEGATION_CONTROL, UPDATE_DELEGATION_CONTROL, and DELETE_DELEGATION_CONTROL operation types plus CREATED, UPDATED, DELETED, IN_PROGRESS, RELATED, and FAILED resource actions. The reviewed runtime uses service-sdk GetWorkRequest polling and shared async status instead of provider helper waiters and keeps delete confirmation repo-authored through GetDelegationControl/ListDelegationControls.

VaultId and VaultKeyId remain create-time inputs that are only valid when resourceType is CLOUDVMCLUSTER. The published runtime keeps that constraint explicit and never attempts in-place vault reconfiguration.
